articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious 'Green Hydrogen Export Hub' Strategy, Targets Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a comprehensive strategy to position India as a leading global exporter of Green Hydrogen and its derivatives by 2030.
• The strategy includes enhanced financial incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on both production and domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
• Special economic zones and dedicated industrial corridors are planned to facilitate large-scale green hydrogen production and export infrastructure.
• Emphasis is placed on research and development for cost reduction and scaling up new applications in sectors like steel, fertilizers, and mobility.
• The move aims to boost energy security, create jobs, and contribute significantly to India's net-zero targets.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Economic Development
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's target: Global Green Hydrogen export hub by 2030.
National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Green Hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Applications include fertilizers, refining, steel, shipping, and power generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategic rationale for becoming a Green Hydrogen export hub, linking it to energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges (e.g., cost, infrastructure, technology) associated with scaling up green hydrogen production.
Evaluate the role of government policies and international collaborations in achieving India's green hydrogen ambitions.
Examine the environmental benefits and potential socio-economic impacts of this initiative.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Hyderabad Building Fire Claims Six Lives, Prompts City-Wide Safety Audit
articles.explainer:
• A devastating fire erupted in a four-story residential-cum-commercial building in Hyderabad, Telangana, resulting in the tragic death of six individuals.
• Preliminary investigations suggest an electrical short circuit as the cause, though a full inquiry is underway.
• The incident has highlighted concerns regarding adherence to fire safety norms and building codes in densely populated urban areas.
• Telangana government has ordered an immediate city-wide fire safety audit of all multi-story buildings, particularly older structures and those with mixed occupancy.
• The disaster management authority emphasized the need for regular drills and public awareness campaigns on fire prevention and evacuation procedures.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and Disaster Management
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Location: Hyderabad, Telangana.
Cause: Suspected electrical short circuit.
Government response: City-wide fire safety audit ordered.
Focus: Multi-story buildings, older structures, mixed-use occupancy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in enforcing fire safety regulations in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Suggest comprehensive measures for improving urban fire safety, including technological solutions, policy reforms, and public awareness.
Analyze the role of various stakeholders (government, builders, residents) in ensuring compliance with building codes and disaster preparedness.
Examine the socio-economic impacts of such urban disasters on vulnerable populations.
articles.imageDescription: Firefighters battling a blaze in a multi-story building in a crowded urban area.
articles.trivia: India's National Building Code (NBC) provides guidelines for regulating building construction activities, including fire safety measures.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Legality of Electoral Bonds Scheme
articles.explainer:
• A five-judge Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court of India began hearing arguments on a batch of petitions challenging the legality of the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme infringes upon the citizens' Right to Information and promotes opaque political funding, leading to quid pro quo corruption.
• The government has defended the scheme, citing its aim to curb black money in elections and ensure donor anonymity.
• The hearings are expected to delve into the balance between transparency in political funding and donor privacy.
• The outcome could significantly impact the landscape of political financing in India ahead of upcoming elections.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds scheme introduced in 2017 through Finance Act, 2017.
Challenges relate to Right to Information (Article 19(1)(a)).
Scheme allows anonymous donations to political parties via specific bank branches.
Arguments against: lack of transparency, potential for corruption.
Arguments for: curbing black money, protecting donor privacy.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds scheme in the context of democratic transparency and political funding.
Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to elections and political finance.
Suggest alternative reforms to ensure transparent and ethical political funding in India.
Analyze how opaque funding mechanisms can impact the integrity of the electoral process and governance.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a gavel in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Critical Technologies and Indo-Pacific Security
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming commitment to a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
• Key agreements were reached on enhancing cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, including semiconductors, AI, and quantum computing.
• Discussions also focused on strengthening maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, emphasizing a free and open international order.
• Both nations reiterated their support for Quad initiatives and multilateral cooperation to address regional and global challenges.
• The dialogue also saw progress on economic partnerships, infrastructure development, and clean energy projects.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan: 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Focus areas: Semiconductors, AI, Quantum Computing, Maritime Security.
Indo-Pacific strategy: Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP).
Quad members: India, Japan, USA, Australia.
Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure projects (e.g., High-Speed Rail).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan cooperation in shaping the geopolitical and geoeconomic landscape of the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss how collaboration in critical technologies can enhance India's strategic autonomy and economic competitiveness.
Evaluate the role of bilateral and multilateral frameworks (like Quad) in addressing security challenges and promoting regional stability.
Examine the potential for India-Japan partnership to contribute to global supply chain resilience and technology governance.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is the only country in the world to have an emperor as its head of state.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Cites Easing Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• The decision was driven by a careful assessment of moderating domestic inflation, although retail inflation remains within the target band.
• The MPC noted persistent global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key risks to the economic outlook.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate actions dependent on evolving inflation and growth dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
Primary objective of MPC: maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target band in India: 4% with a +/- 2% tolerance band (2-6%).
Retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation in India.
Examine the impact of global geopolitical events and commodity prices on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for credit growth and investment.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with a graph showing stable interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Phase of 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' Focused on Digital Literacy for Girls
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has launched an expanded phase of the 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' (BBBP) scheme.
• This new phase specifically targets enhancing digital literacy and STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) education among adolescent girls in rural and semi-urban areas.
• The initiative aims to bridge the digital divide and empower girls with skills crucial for future employment and participation in the digital economy.
• Partnerships with educational institutions, NGOs, and technology companies are central to the implementation strategy.
• The scheme also continues its focus on preventing gender-biased sex selection and ensuring survival, protection, and education of the girl child.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: BBBP scheme launched in 2015.
Initial focus: improving Child Sex Ratio (CSR), promoting girls' education.
New phase adds digital literacy and STEM education for girls.
Implementing Ministry: Women and Child Development.
Aims to empower girls for the digital economy.
mainsPoints: Assess the effectiveness of 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' in achieving its original objectives and the potential impact of its expanded focus on digital literacy.
Discuss the importance of digital literacy for women's empowerment in the 21st century and the challenges in its widespread implementation in rural India.
Analyze how integrating STEM education into gender equality initiatives can contribute to India's human capital development and economic growth.
Suggest measures to ensure inclusive access to digital education for all girls, particularly those from marginalized communities.
articles.imageDescription: Group of young girls learning on tablets in a rural setting.
articles.trivia: The 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' scheme was initially launched from Panipat, Haryana.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Re-Entry Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful testing of a critical technology demonstrator for its next-generation re-entry vehicle.
• This test marks a significant step towards developing fully reusable launch vehicles and advanced space exploration missions.
• The demonstrator showcased capabilities in autonomous navigation, guidance, and control during atmospheric re-entry, crucial for safe and precise landings.
• The technology is expected to reduce the cost of space access and enhance India's capabilities in orbital mechanics and planetary missions.
• This achievement is part of ISRO's long-term vision for sustainable and cost-effective space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO: Indian Space Research Organisation.
Test involved: Next-generation re-entry vehicle technology demonstrator.
Significance: Towards reusable launch vehicles and advanced missions.
Key capabilities demonstrated: Autonomous navigation, guidance, and control during re-entry.
Aims to reduce cost of space access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's future space missions and its competitive edge in the global space industry.
Analyze how advancements in re-entry technology contribute to both scientific exploration and commercial space applications.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in developing indigenous capabilities for advanced space technologies.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic benefits of reduced space access costs for India.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of an ISRO re-entry vehicle descending through Earth's atmosphere.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: BSF Foils Infiltration Bid Along Punjab Border, Recovers Narcotics and Arms
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) successfully thwarted an infiltration attempt by a group of armed smugglers along the India-Pakistan border in the Punjab sector.
• During the operation, BSF personnel engaged the infiltrators, forcing them to retreat back across the border.
• A thorough search operation in the area led to the recovery of a significant consignment of narcotics, along with arms and ammunition.
• The incident highlights the persistent challenges of cross-border smuggling and illicit activities in the region.
• BSF officials reiterated their commitment to maintaining strict vigilance and enhancing surveillance technologies to secure the international border.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding force for land borders.
Mandate: Guarding India's land borders during peacetime and preventing transnational crime.
Punjab shares an international border with Pakistan.
Challenges in border areas: infiltration, smuggling of arms and drugs.
BSF operates under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges faced by border guarding forces like BSF in managing India's porous land borders, particularly in the context of cross-border smuggling and infiltration.
Analyze the nexus between narcotics trafficking, arms smuggling, and terrorism in border regions and its implications for India's internal security.
Suggest technological and strategic measures to enhance border security and surveillance capabilities.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to cross-border illicit activities in states like Punjab and potential solutions.
articles.imageDescription: BSF personnel patrolling along a border fence in a rural area.
articles.trivia: The BSF was raised in 1965 in the aftermath of the 1965 Indo-Pak War.

articles.headline: New Study Reveals Accelerated Glacial Retreat in Himalayas, Threatening Regional Water Security
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive study published by a consortium of Indian and international scientists indicates an alarming rate of glacial retreat across the Himalayan range.
• The research, utilizing satellite imagery and ground-based observations, shows that glaciers are melting at an unprecedented pace, exceeding previous projections.
• This accelerated melting poses a severe long-term threat to water security for millions of people dependent on Himalayan rivers for drinking water, agriculture, and hydropower.
• The study attributes the retreat primarily to rising global temperatures and localized climate change impacts.
• Experts call for urgent regional cooperation and enhanced climate adaptation strategies to mitigate the impending water crisis.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Himalayan glaciers are a major source of water for several major Asian rivers (e.g., Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra).
Study indicates accelerated glacial retreat.
Primary cause: rising global temperatures / climate change.
Impact: long-term threat to water security for millions.
Need for: regional cooperation and climate adaptation strategies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-dimensional impacts of accelerated glacial retreat in the Himalayas on India's water resources, agriculture, and energy security.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective climate adaptation and mitigation strategies in the Himalayan region.
Examine the need for regional cooperation among riparian states to address the transboundary implications of glacial melt.
Suggest policy interventions and technological solutions to enhance water resource management and build resilience against climate change impacts.
articles.imageDescription: Panoramic view of melting glaciers in the Himalayas with sparse vegetation.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for ACC Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investments, Boosts EV Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• Several major players have committed to setting up Giga factories in India, indicating robust confidence in the country's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem.
• This initiative is crucial for reducing India's dependence on battery imports and establishing a resilient domestic supply chain for EVs.
• The scheme aims to boost indigenous manufacturing capabilities, create jobs, and accelerate India's transition to green mobility.
• Government officials highlighted the potential for India to become a global hub for ACC battery production, leveraging its growing EV market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC batteries is part of India's broader PLI initiative.
Objective: Reduce import dependence, boost domestic manufacturing of batteries.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
Aims to make India a global manufacturing hub for ACCs.
Scheme falls under the Ministry of Heavy Industries.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the PLI scheme in attracting investments and fostering indigenous manufacturing capabilities in critical sectors like ACC battery production.
Discuss the role of the EV sector in India's energy transition and economic growth, highlighting the challenges and opportunities.
Analyze how government incentives like PLI can contribute to building resilient domestic supply chains and reducing strategic dependencies.
Examine the environmental and economic benefits of promoting green mobility and localizing battery production.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of an electric car charging station with a battery manufacturing plant in the background.
articles.trivia: India aims for 30% of new car sales to be electric by 2030.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive UNSC Reforms at Multilateral Forum
articles.explainer:
• India strongly advocated for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a high-level multilateral forum.
• The Indian representative emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect the contemporary geopolitical realities of the 21st century.
• India called for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership to make the Council more representative, effective, and legitimate.
• The statement highlighted India's consistent demand for a permanent seat, citing its growing economic and strategic influence, and its commitment to multilateralism.
• The discussion underscored the urgent need to address the long-pending issue of UNSC reforms to enhance global governance.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5) and 10 non-permanent members.
P5 members: China, France, Russia, UK, USA (all have veto power).
Non-permanent members are elected for two-year terms.
India advocates for expansion in both permanent and non-permanent categories.
India is a member of G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) seeking permanent UNSC seats.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the arguments for and against the expansion of the UNSC, particularly concerning India's bid for a permanent seat.
Discuss how the current structure of the UNSC impacts its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges.
Examine the implications of UNSC reforms for global power dynamics and India's role in the international order.
Suggest a roadmap for achieving meaningful UNSC reforms, considering the interests of various member states.
articles.imageDescription: United Nations headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council held its first session on January 17, 1946, in London.

articles.headline: New Guidelines Issued for Sustainable Management of E-Waste in India
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released updated guidelines for the sustainable management and disposal of electronic waste (e-waste).
• The revised framework emphasizes extended producer responsibility (EPR), mandating manufacturers to collect and recycle a higher percentage of their products.
• It introduces stricter compliance mechanisms, digital tracking of e-waste, and incentives for formal recycling units.
• The guidelines aim to address the growing challenge of e-waste generation, promote a circular economy, and prevent environmental pollution.
• Public awareness campaigns will also be launched to encourage responsible disposal of electronic goods by consumers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: E-waste (Management) Rules first notified in 2011, then 2016, 2022.
New guidelines focus on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
EPR makes producers responsible for lifecycle management of products.
Aims to formalize e-waste recycling sector and prevent informal dumping.
Promotes circular economy principles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing challenge of e-waste generation in India and its environmental and health implications.
Critically evaluate the 'Extended Producer Responsibility' (EPR) concept as a tool for sustainable e-waste management, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses.
Suggest measures to strengthen the formal e-waste recycling sector and integrate the informal sector into a regulated framework.
Analyze the role of technology and public awareness in promoting responsible e-waste disposal and a circular economy.
articles.imageDescription: Pile of discarded electronic devices, symbolizing e-waste.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Speaker Emphasizes Need for Greater Parliamentary Decorum Ahead of Budget Session
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha Speaker addressed leaders of all political parties, stressing the importance of maintaining decorum and constructive debate in Parliament.
• The appeal comes ahead of the crucial Budget Session, where significant legislative business and financial discussions are expected.
• The Speaker highlighted growing public concern over frequent disruptions and unparliamentary conduct, which undermine the dignity of the institution.
• Discussions focused on finding common ground to ensure smooth functioning of both Houses and facilitating meaningful legislative outcomes.
• Party leaders largely agreed on the need for cooperation, while also emphasizing the opposition's right to protest and hold the government accountable.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Lok Sabha Speaker presides over the Lower House of Parliament.
Budget Session is typically the longest parliamentary session.
Role of Speaker: maintain order and decorum, interpret rules.
Unparliamentary conduct: actions or words deemed inappropriate for parliamentary proceedings.
Disruptions impact legislative productivity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the declining standards of parliamentary decorum in India and its implications for democratic governance and public trust.
Analyze the causes of frequent disruptions in Parliament, including the role of both ruling and opposition parties.
Suggest reforms and measures to enhance the productivity and dignity of parliamentary proceedings, drawing comparisons with international best practices.
Examine the Speaker's role in upholding parliamentary traditions and ensuring constructive debate.
articles.imageDescription: Interior of the Indian Parliament building during a session.
articles.trivia: The first Lok Sabha was constituted on 17 April 1952.

articles.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates National Conference on Preserving Intangible Cultural Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Culture inaugurated a national conference focused on strategies for the preservation and promotion of India's rich intangible cultural heritage (ICH).
• The conference brought together experts, academicians, artists, and policymakers to discuss challenges and best practices in safeguarding traditions, oral expressions, performing arts, and traditional craftsmanship.
• Emphasis was placed on community participation and leveraging digital technologies for documentation and dissemination of ICH.
• The Ministry announced new funding initiatives and skill development programs to support practitioners of traditional arts and crafts.
• The event underscored India's commitment to the UNESCO Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Intangible cultural heritage (ICH) includes traditions, oral expressions, performing arts, social practices, rituals, festive events, knowledge and practices concerning nature and the universe, and traditional craftsmanship.
UNESCO Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage (2003).
India has several ICH elements inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List (e.g., Yoga, Kumbh Mela, Durga Puja).
Ministry of Culture is the nodal ministry.
Focus on documentation, digital preservation, and community involvement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of preserving India's intangible cultural heritage in fostering national identity and promoting cultural diversity.
Analyze the challenges faced in safeguarding ICH in the modern era, including globalization, urbanization, and changing lifestyles.
Evaluate the role of government policies, technological interventions, and community-led initiatives in the effective preservation and promotion of ICH.
Examine how ICH can be leveraged for sustainable tourism and economic empowerment of local communities.
articles.imageDescription: Artists performing a traditional Indian dance form on stage.
articles.trivia: Koodiyattam, a traditional theatre art form from Kerala, was the first Indian ICH element inscribed by UNESCO in 2008.

articles.headline: Agricultural Exports See Robust Growth, Driven by Diversification and Value Addition
articles.explainer:
• India's agricultural exports registered robust growth in the past financial year, driven by strategic diversification and focus on value-added products.
• The Department of Commerce reported significant increases in the export of processed foods, fruits, vegetables, and non-basmati rice.
• Government initiatives, including the Agri Export Policy and various export promotion schemes, have played a crucial role in this success.
• Efforts to improve logistics, cold chain infrastructure, and compliance with international quality standards have also contributed to better market access.
• The growth is seen as a positive step towards enhancing farmers' income and strengthening India's position in global agricultural trade.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India is a major global agricultural producer.
Agri Export Policy aims to double agricultural exports by 2022 (original target), diversify export basket.
Focus on processed foods, fruits, vegetables, non-basmati rice.
Key factors: improved logistics, cold chain, quality standards.
APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) facilitates agri-exports.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of agricultural exports in boosting farmers' income and contributing to India's overall economic growth.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in diversifying India's agricultural export basket and enhancing value addition in the food processing sector.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and infrastructure development in promoting agri-exports and ensuring compliance with international standards.
Examine the potential for India to become a global leader in specific agricultural product categories and the strategies required.
articles.imageDescription: Farmer inspecting crops in a field, with a cargo ship in the background symbolizing exports.
articles.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of milk, pulses, and spices.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Disease
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists from the National Institute of Immunology (NII) and other research institutions announced a significant breakthrough in developing a vaccine for a neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The experimental vaccine has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• NTDs disproportionately affect impoverished communities in tropical and subtropical regions, including parts of India.
• This development underscores India's growing capabilities in biomedical research and its commitment to addressing global health challenges.
• Further clinical trials are planned, with hopes for a cost-effective and accessible vaccine to combat the disease.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical/subtropical regions.
They primarily affect low-income populations.
Examples of NTDs: Dengue, Chikungunya, Kala-azar, Lymphatic Filariasis, Rabies.
Indian scientists achieved breakthrough in vaccine development.
National Institute of Immunology (NII) is a key research body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and the challenges in their control and elimination.
Analyze the significance of indigenous vaccine development in strengthening India's public health infrastructure and achieving health equity.
Examine the role of scientific research and international collaboration in accelerating the development of affordable and accessible treatments for NTDs.
Evaluate government strategies and policies required to integrate new vaccines into national health programs effectively.
articles.imageDescription: Microscope and laboratory equipment in a research facility.
articles.trivia: World Neglected Tropical Diseases Day is observed on January 30th each year.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies Directed to Strengthen Flood Preparedness and Early Warning Systems
articles.explainer:
• The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) issued fresh directives to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country to bolster urban flood preparedness.
• The directive emphasizes the urgent need for robust early warning systems, particularly integrating AI and IoT for real-time monitoring of rainfall and water levels.
• ULBs are mandated to conduct vulnerability assessments, clear drainage systems, and identify flood-prone areas for pre-emptive action.
• Community participation through local disaster response teams and public awareness campaigns is also a key component of the strategy.
• The move comes in anticipation of increased extreme weather events and aims to minimize loss of life and property during monsoons.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and Disaster Management
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: NDMA is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Focus on urban flood preparedness due to increasing extreme weather events.
Key elements: early warning systems (AI/IoT), drainage clearance, vulnerability assessment.
Importance of community participation and public awareness.
Urban flooding is a significant challenge in Indian cities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes and consequences of urban flooding in India, considering factors like climate change, unplanned urbanization, and inadequate infrastructure.
Discuss the effectiveness of current disaster management frameworks in addressing urban flood challenges and suggest improvements.
Examine the role of technology (AI, IoT) and community engagement in enhancing urban flood preparedness and response.
Propose a comprehensive strategy for building climate-resilient urban infrastructure and improving drainage systems in Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: Waterlogged city street during a monsoon, with people wading through floodwaters.
articles.trivia: The National Disaster Management Act was enacted in India in 2005.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Attention as a Standard for Inclusivity
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is receiving widespread international recognition.
• Several developing nations are exploring adopting similar frameworks to accelerate financial inclusion and digital governance.
• The G20 platform has been instrumental in showcasing India's DPI success, highlighting its scalability, interoperability, and cost-effectiveness.
• Experts emphasize that the model offers a blueprint for building inclusive, open, and secure digital ecosystems globally.
• India is actively engaging in knowledge sharing and technical assistance to help other countries implement their own DPIs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: India's DPI includes Aadhaar (digital identity), UPI (payments), DigiLocker (document storage).
Key features: scalability, interoperability, cost-effectiveness.
G20 platform used to showcase India's DPI model.
Aimed at accelerating financial inclusion and digital governance.
India is providing technical assistance to other nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the components and foundational principles of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its role in fostering inclusive growth and digital transformation.
Discuss the opportunities and challenges for other developing nations in adopting India's DPI model, considering aspects like data privacy, cybersecurity, and local contexts.
Evaluate the impact of DPI on financial inclusion, public service delivery, and economic development in India.
Examine India's role in promoting global digital public goods and shaping international standards for digital governance.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Support for Startup Ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) announced enhanced support measures for startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The initiative includes increased funding for incubators, mentorship programs, and easier access to venture capital for entrepreneurs outside major metropolitan areas.
• The aim is to decentralize the startup ecosystem, tap into local talent, and promote inclusive economic growth across India.
• Special focus will be given to startups in emerging sectors like AgriTech, HealthTech, and sustainable technologies relevant to regional needs.
• Workshops and hackathons will be organized to foster innovation and entrepreneurial spirit in smaller cities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.

Analyze the challenges faced by entrepreneurs in smaller cities compared to metropolitan hubs and how government policies can address them.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Discuss Roadmap for Enhanced Climate Finance Mobilization
articles.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors convened virtually to discuss a roadmap for significantly enhancing climate finance mobilization.
• The discussions focused on achieving the global goal of mobilizing $100 billion annually for climate action in developing countries.
• Key proposals included innovative financing mechanisms, greater private sector involvement, and reforms of Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs).
• India, emphasizing common but differentiated responsibilities, pushed for increased grant-based financing and technology transfer to developing nations.
• The meeting underscored the urgency of climate finance in achieving global climate targets and supporting vulnerable economies.

mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in mobilizing climate finance globally, particularly for developing countries.
Discuss India's stance on climate finance, including the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR) and its implications for international negotiations.
Analyze the role of Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) and the private sector in scaling up climate investments.
Suggest innovative mechanisms and policy reforms to bridge the climate finance gap and accelerate climate action in vulnerable nations.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo superimposed on an image of a globe with renewable energy sources.

articles.headline: National Voter Awareness Campaign Launched to Boost Electoral Participation
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) launched a nationwide voter awareness campaign to encourage greater participation in the upcoming electoral cycles.
• The campaign focuses on educating citizens, especially first-time voters and marginalized groups, about the importance of voting and electoral procedures.
• Utilizing digital platforms, social media, and community outreach programs, the ECI aims to reach a diverse demographic.
• Special emphasis is placed on ensuring accessible voting for persons with disabilities and senior citizens.
• The initiative reiterates the ECI's commitment to free, fair, and inclusive elections across the country.

highlights: India launches ambitious 'Green Hydrogen Export Hub' strategy, aiming for global leadership by 2030 with new incentives and dedicated infrastructure.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a leading global exporter of Green Hydrogen by 2030.
2. The mission provides financial incentives primarily for the import of electrolysers to meet domestic demand.
3. Green Hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources for the electrolysis of water.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission aims to position India as a global hub for Green Hydrogen exports by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect. The mission focuses on providing financial incentives for *domestic manufacturing* of electrolysers to reduce import dependence and boost indigenous capabilities. Statement 3 is correct. Green Hydrogen is defined by its production method, which uses renewable energy to power the electrolysis of water, resulting in minimal carbon emissions.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is a six-member committee responsible for determining the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target within the specified tolerance band. The repo rate is a key policy rate.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' scheme, recently expanded to include digital literacy, is implemented by which Union Ministry?
options: (b) Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
(c) Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' (BBBP) scheme is a flagship initiative of the Ministry of Women and Child Development, launched to address issues of declining Child Sex Ratio and promote girls' education and empowerment. Its new phase includes a focus on digital literacy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are key components of India's DPI.
2. The DPI model primarily focuses on urban areas to accelerate digital transformation.
3. India is sharing its DPI model with other developing nations to foster financial inclusion.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. Aadhaar (digital identity), UPI (payments), and DigiLocker (secure document wallet) are foundational elements of India's DPI. Statement 2 is incorrect. The DPI model aims for inclusive growth and digital transformation across both urban and rural areas. Statement 3 is correct. India has been actively showcasing and assisting other developing nations in adopting similar DPI frameworks, particularly through platforms like the G20.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a permanent member of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The five permanent members (P5) of the UNSC are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States. Germany is a member of the G4 nations (along with India, Brazil, and Japan) that advocate for permanent seats in the UNSC, but it is not currently a permanent member.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition to become a global Green Hydrogen export hub presents significant opportunities and challenges. Critically analyze the strategic rationale behind this ambition and discuss the key policy interventions required to achieve it.

mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's Green Hydrogen ambition and its significance. Discuss the strategic rationale (energy security, climate goals, economic growth, 'Make in India'). Elaborate on opportunities (job creation, export potential, technological advancement). Address challenges (cost, infrastructure, R&D, international competition). Suggest policy interventions (incentives, R&D funding, infrastructure development, regulatory framework, international collaboration). Conclude with a balanced outlook.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's review of the Electoral Bonds scheme highlights a perennial debate between transparency and donor privacy in political funding. mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Electoral Bonds scheme and the Supreme Court's review. Discuss the constitutional arguments against the scheme (Right to Information, transparency, potential for quid pro quo corruption) and arguments for it (curbing black money, donor anonymity). Analyze the impact on democratic processes. Propose comprehensive reforms (e.g., state funding, cap on corporate donations, independent audit, digital traceability, strengthening ECI). Conclude on the importance of transparent political funding for a robust democracy.

mainsMocks.question: Rapid glacial retreat in the Himalayas poses an existential threat to water security in the Indian subcontinent. Examine the causes and multi-dimensional consequences of this phenomenon and suggest a holistic strategy for climate adaptation and water resource management in the region.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Important Geophysical phenomena; changes in critical geographical features; GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; Disaster and Disaster Management
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the severity of Himalayan glacial retreat and its impact on water security. Discuss the primary causes (global warming, black carbon, localized factors). Detail multi-dimensional consequences (water scarcity, agricultural impact, hydropower, extreme weather events, ecological disruption). Propose a holistic strategy (regional cooperation, climate adaptation measures, sustainable water resource management, early warning systems, afforestation, public awareness, research and monitoring). Conclude on the urgency and collaborative effort needed.

GS1.headline: Government Launches New Phase of 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' Focused on Digital Literacy for Girls
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has launched an expanded phase of the 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' (BBBP) scheme.
• This new phase specifically targets enhancing digital literacy and STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) education among adolescent girls in rural and semi-urban areas.
• The initiative aims to bridge the digital divide and empower girls with skills crucial for future employment and participation in the digital economy.
• Partnerships with educational institutions, NGOs, and technology companies are central to the implementation strategy.
• The scheme also continues its focus on preventing gender-biased sex selection and ensuring survival, protection, and education of the girl child.

Analyze how integrating STEM education into gender equality initiatives can contribute to India's human capital development and economic growth.
Suggest measures to ensure inclusive access to digital education for all girls, particularly those from marginalized communities.
GS1.imageDescription: Group of young girls learning on tablets in a rural setting.
GS1.trivia: The 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' scheme was initially launched from Panipat, Haryana.

GS1.headline: New Study Reveals Accelerated Glacial Retreat in Himalayas, Threatening Regional Water Security
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive study published by a consortium of Indian and international scientists indicates an alarming rate of glacial retreat across the Himalayan range.
• The research, utilizing satellite imagery and ground-based observations, shows that glaciers are melting at an unprecedented pace, exceeding previous projections.
• This accelerated melting poses a severe long-term threat to water security for millions of people dependent on Himalayan rivers for drinking water, agriculture, and hydropower.
• The study attributes the retreat primarily to rising global temperatures and localized climate change impacts.
• Experts call for urgent regional cooperation and enhanced climate adaptation strategies to mitigate the impending water crisis.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

prelimsPoints: Himalayan glaciers are a major source of water for several major Asian rivers (e.g., Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra).
Study indicates accelerated glacial retreat.
Primary cause: rising global temperatures / climate change.
Impact: long-term threat to water security for millions.
Need for: regional cooperation and climate adaptation strategies.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-dimensional impacts of accelerated glacial retreat in the Himalayas on India's water resources, agriculture, and energy security.
Discuss the challenges in implementing effective climate adaptation and mitigation strategies in the Himalayan region.
Examine the need for regional cooperation among riparian states to address the transboundary implications of glacial melt.
Suggest policy interventions and technological solutions to enhance water resource management and build resilience against climate change impacts.
GS1.imageDescription: Panoramic view of melting glaciers in the Himalayas with sparse vegetation.
GS1.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

GS1.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates National Conference on Preserving Intangible Cultural Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Union Minister for Culture inaugurated a national conference focused on strategies for the preservation and promotion of India's rich intangible cultural heritage (ICH).
• The conference brought together experts, academicians, artists, and policymakers to discuss challenges and best practices in safeguarding traditions, oral expressions, performing arts, and traditional craftsmanship.
• Emphasis was placed on community participation and leveraging digital technologies for documentation and dissemination of ICH.
• The Ministry announced new funding initiatives and skill development programs to support practitioners of traditional arts and crafts.
• The event underscored India's commitment to the UNESCO Convention for the Safeguarding of the Intangible Cultural Heritage.

Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Legality of Electoral Bonds Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• A five-judge Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court of India began hearing arguments on a batch of petitions challenging the legality of the Electoral Bonds scheme.
• Petitioners argue that the scheme infringes upon the citizens' Right to Information and promotes opaque political funding, leading to quid pro quo corruption.
• The government has defended the scheme, citing its aim to curb black money in elections and ensure donor anonymity.
• The hearings are expected to delve into the balance between transparency in political funding and donor privacy.
• The outcome could significantly impact the landscape of political financing in India ahead of upcoming elections.

Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.

Challenges relate to Right to Information (Article 19(1)(a)).
Scheme allows anonymous donations to political parties via specific bank branches.

Arguments for: curbing black money, protecting donor privacy.

Discuss the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to elections and political finance.
Suggest alternative reforms to ensure transparent and ethical political funding in India.
Analyze how opaque funding mechanisms can impact the integrity of the electoral process and governance.

• Key agreements were reached on enhancing cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, including semiconductors, AI, and quantum computing.
• Discussions also focused on strengthening maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, emphasizing a free and open international order.
• Both nations reiterated their support for Quad initiatives and multilateral cooperation to address regional and global challenges.
• The dialogue also saw progress on economic partnerships, infrastructure development, and clean energy projects.

• The Indian representative emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect the contemporary geopolitical realities of the 21st century.
• India called for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent categories of membership to make the Council more representative, effective, and legitimate.
• The statement highlighted India's consistent demand for a permanent seat, citing its growing economic and strategic influence, and its commitment to multilateralism.
• The discussion underscored the urgent need to address the long-pending issue of UNSC reforms to enhance global governance.

• The strategy includes enhanced financial incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on both production and domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
• Special economic zones and dedicated industrial corridors are planned to facilitate large-scale green hydrogen production and export infrastructure.
• Emphasis is placed on research and development for cost reduction and scaling up new applications in sectors like steel, fertilizers, and mobility.
• The move aims to boost energy security, create jobs, and contribute significantly to India's net-zero targets.

• Preliminary investigations suggest an electrical short circuit as the cause, though a full inquiry is underway.
• The incident has highlighted concerns regarding adherence to fire safety norms and building codes in densely populated urban areas.
• Telangana government has ordered an immediate city-wide fire safety audit of all multi-story buildings, particularly older structures and those with mixed occupancy.
• The disaster management authority emphasized the need for regular drills and public awareness campaigns on fire prevention and evacuation procedures.

Cause: Suspected electrical short circuit.
Government response: City-wide fire safety audit ordered.
Focus: Multi-story buildings, older structures, mixed-use occupancy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in enforcing fire safety regulations in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Suggest comprehensive measures for improving urban fire safety, including technological solutions, policy reforms, and public awareness.
Analyze the role of various stakeholders (government, builders, residents) in ensuring compliance with building codes and disaster preparedness.
Examine the socio-economic impacts of such urban disasters on vulnerable populations.
GS3.imageDescription: Firefighters battling a blaze in a multi-story building in a crowded urban area.
GS3.trivia: India's National Building Code (NBC) provides guidelines for regulating building construction activities, including fire safety measures.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Cites Easing Inflationary Pressures and Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• The decision was driven by a careful assessment of moderating domestic inflation, although retail inflation remains within the target band.
• The MPC noted persistent global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key risks to the economic outlook.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued vigilance from the central bank, with future rate actions dependent on evolving inflation and growth dynamics.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate in India.
Primary objective of MPC: maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target band in India: 4% with a +/- 2% tolerance band (2-6%).
Retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation in India.
Examine the impact of global geopolitical events and commodity prices on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.
Evaluate the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance and its implications for credit growth and investment.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with a graph showing stable interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Re-Entry Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful testing of a critical technology demonstrator for its next-generation re-entry vehicle.
• This test marks a significant step towards developing fully reusable launch vehicles and advanced space exploration missions.
• The demonstrator showcased capabilities in autonomous navigation, guidance, and control during atmospheric re-entry, crucial for safe and precise landings.
• The technology is expected to reduce the cost of space access and enhance India's capabilities in orbital mechanics and planetary missions.
• This achievement is part of ISRO's long-term vision for sustainable and cost-effective space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO: Indian Space Research Organisation.
Test involved: Next-generation re-entry vehicle technology demonstrator.
Significance: Towards reusable launch vehicles and advanced missions.
Key capabilities demonstrated: Autonomous navigation, guidance, and control during re-entry.
Aims to reduce cost of space access.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's future space missions and its competitive edge in the global space industry.
Analyze how advancements in re-entry technology contribute to both scientific exploration and commercial space applications.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in developing indigenous capabilities for advanced space technologies.
Evaluate the potential socio-economic benefits of reduced space access costs for India.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of an ISRO re-entry vehicle descending through Earth's atmosphere.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: BSF Foils Infiltration Bid Along Punjab Border, Recovers Narcotics and Arms
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) successfully thwarted an infiltration attempt by a group of armed smugglers along the India-Pakistan border in the Punjab sector.
• During the operation, BSF personnel engaged the infiltrators, forcing them to retreat back across the border.
• A thorough search operation in the area led to the recovery of a significant consignment of narcotics, along with arms and ammunition.
• The incident highlights the persistent challenges of cross-border smuggling and illicit activities in the region.
• BSF officials reiterated their commitment to maintaining strict vigilance and enhancing surveillance technologies to secure the international border.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: BSF is India's primary border guarding force for land borders.
Mandate: Guarding India's land borders during peacetime and preventing transnational crime.
Punjab shares an international border with Pakistan.
Challenges in border areas: infiltration, smuggling of arms and drugs.
BSF operates under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges faced by border guarding forces like BSF in managing India's porous land borders, particularly in the context of cross-border smuggling and infiltration.
Analyze the nexus between narcotics trafficking, arms smuggling, and terrorism in border regions and its implications for India's internal security.
Suggest technological and strategic measures to enhance border security and surveillance capabilities.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to cross-border illicit activities in states like Punjab and potential solutions.
GS3.imageDescription: BSF personnel patrolling along a border fence in a rural area.
GS3.trivia: The BSF was raised in 1965 in the aftermath of the 1965 Indo-Pak War.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for ACC Battery Manufacturing Attracts Major Investments, Boosts EV Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing has successfully attracted significant domestic and international investments.
• Several major players have committed to setting up Giga factories in India, indicating robust confidence in the country's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem.
• This initiative is crucial for reducing India's dependence on battery imports and establishing a resilient domestic supply chain for EVs.
• The scheme aims to boost indigenous manufacturing capabilities, create jobs, and accelerate India's transition to green mobility.
• Government officials highlighted the potential for India to become a global hub for ACC battery production, leveraging its growing EV market.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC batteries is part of India's broader PLI initiative.
Objective: Reduce import dependence, boost domestic manufacturing of batteries.
ACC batteries are crucial for electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.
Aims to make India a global manufacturing hub for ACCs.
Scheme falls under the Ministry of Heavy Industries.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the PLI scheme in attracting investments and fostering indigenous manufacturing capabilities in critical sectors like ACC battery production.
Discuss the role of the EV sector in India's energy transition and economic growth, highlighting the challenges and opportunities.
Analyze how government incentives like PLI can contribute to building resilient domestic supply chains and reducing strategic dependencies.
Examine the environmental and economic benefits of promoting green mobility and localizing battery production.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of an electric car charging station with a battery manufacturing plant in the background.
GS3.trivia: India aims for 30% of new car sales to be electric by 2030.

GS3.headline: New Guidelines Issued for Sustainable Management of E-Waste in India
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has released updated guidelines for the sustainable management and disposal of electronic waste (e-waste).
• The revised framework emphasizes extended producer responsibility (EPR), mandating manufacturers to collect and recycle a higher percentage of their products.
• It introduces stricter compliance mechanisms, digital tracking of e-waste, and incentives for formal recycling units.
• The guidelines aim to address the growing challenge of e-waste generation, promote a circular economy, and prevent environmental pollution.
• Public awareness campaigns will also be launched to encourage responsible disposal of electronic goods by consumers.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: E-waste (Management) Rules first notified in 2011, then 2016, 2022.
New guidelines focus on Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
EPR makes producers responsible for lifecycle management of products.
Aims to formalize e-waste recycling sector and prevent informal dumping.
Promotes circular economy principles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing challenge of e-waste generation in India and its environmental and health implications.
Critically evaluate the 'Extended Producer Responsibility' (EPR) concept as a tool for sustainable e-waste management, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses.
Suggest measures to strengthen the formal e-waste recycling sector and integrate the informal sector into a regulated framework.
Analyze the role of technology and public awareness in promoting responsible e-waste disposal and a circular economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Pile of discarded electronic devices, symbolizing e-waste.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world.

GS3.headline: Agricultural Exports See Robust Growth, Driven by Diversification and Value Addition
GS3.explainer:
• India's agricultural exports registered robust growth in the past financial year, driven by strategic diversification and focus on value-added products.
• The Department of Commerce reported significant increases in the export of processed foods, fruits, vegetables, and non-basmati rice.
• Government initiatives, including the Agri Export Policy and various export promotion schemes, have played a crucial role in this success.
• Efforts to improve logistics, cold chain infrastructure, and compliance with international quality standards have also contributed to better market access.
• The growth is seen as a positive step towards enhancing farmers' income and strengthening India's position in global agricultural trade.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India is a major global agricultural producer.
Agri Export Policy aims to double agricultural exports by 2022 (original target), diversify export basket.
Focus on processed foods, fruits, vegetables, non-basmati rice.
Key factors: improved logistics, cold chain, quality standards.
APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) facilitates agri-exports.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of agricultural exports in boosting farmers' income and contributing to India's overall economic growth.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in diversifying India's agricultural export basket and enhancing value addition in the food processing sector.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and infrastructure development in promoting agri-exports and ensuring compliance with international standards.
Examine the potential for India to become a global leader in specific agricultural product categories and the strategies required.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmer inspecting crops in a field, with a cargo ship in the background symbolizing exports.
GS3.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of milk, pulses, and spices.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Disease
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists from the National Institute of Immunology (NII) and other research institutions announced a significant breakthrough in developing a vaccine for a neglected tropical disease (NTD).
• The experimental vaccine has shown promising results in pre-clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• NTDs disproportionately affect impoverished communities in tropical and subtropical regions, including parts of India.
• This development underscores India's growing capabilities in biomedical research and its commitment to addressing global health challenges.
• Further clinical trials are planned, with hopes for a cost-effective and accessible vaccine to combat the disease.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: NTDs are a diverse group of communicable diseases prevalent in tropical/subtropical regions.
They primarily affect low-income populations.
Examples of NTDs: Dengue, Chikungunya, Kala-azar, Lymphatic Filariasis, Rabies.
Indian scientists achieved breakthrough in vaccine development.
National Institute of Immunology (NII) is a key research body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic burden of Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) in India and the challenges in their control and elimination.
Analyze the significance of indigenous vaccine development in strengthening India's public health infrastructure and achieving health equity.
Examine the role of scientific research and international collaboration in accelerating the development of affordable and accessible treatments for NTDs.
Evaluate government strategies and policies required to integrate new vaccines into national health programs effectively.
GS3.imageDescription: Microscope and laboratory equipment in a research facility.
GS3.trivia: World Neglected Tropical Diseases Day is observed on January 30th each year.

GS3.headline: Urban Local Bodies Directed to Strengthen Flood Preparedness and Early Warning Systems
GS3.explainer:
• The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) issued fresh directives to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country to bolster urban flood preparedness.
• The directive emphasizes the urgent need for robust early warning systems, particularly integrating AI and IoT for real-time monitoring of rainfall and water levels.
• ULBs are mandated to conduct vulnerability assessments, clear drainage systems, and identify flood-prone areas for pre-emptive action.
• Community participation through local disaster response teams and public awareness campaigns is also a key component of the strategy.
• The move comes in anticipation of increased extreme weather events and aims to minimize loss of life and property during monsoons.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and Disaster Management
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: NDMA is the apex body for disaster management in India.
Focus on urban flood preparedness due to increasing extreme weather events.
Key elements: early warning systems (AI/IoT), drainage clearance, vulnerability assessment.
Importance of community participation and public awareness.
Urban flooding is a significant challenge in Indian cities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes and consequences of urban flooding in India, considering factors like climate change, unplanned urbanization, and inadequate infrastructure.
Discuss the effectiveness of current disaster management frameworks in addressing urban flood challenges and suggest improvements.
Examine the role of technology (AI, IoT) and community engagement in enhancing urban flood preparedness and response.
Propose a comprehensive strategy for building climate-resilient urban infrastructure and improving drainage systems in Indian cities.
GS3.imageDescription: Waterlogged city street during a monsoon, with people wading through floodwaters.
GS3.trivia: The National Disaster Management Act was enacted in India in 2005.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Garners Global Attention as a Standard for Inclusivity
GS3.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is receiving widespread international recognition.
• Several developing nations are exploring adopting similar frameworks to accelerate financial inclusion and digital governance.
• The G20 platform has been instrumental in showcasing India's DPI success, highlighting its scalability, interoperability, and cost-effectiveness.
• Experts emphasize that the model offers a blueprint for building inclusive, open, and secure digital ecosystems globally.
• India is actively engaging in knowledge sharing and technical assistance to help other countries implement their own DPIs.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: India's DPI includes Aadhaar (digital identity), UPI (payments), DigiLocker (document storage).
Key features: scalability, interoperability, cost-effectiveness.
G20 platform used to showcase India's DPI model.
Aimed at accelerating financial inclusion and digital governance.
India is providing technical assistance to other nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the components and foundational principles of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its role in fostering inclusive growth and digital transformation.
Discuss the opportunities and challenges for other developing nations in adopting India's DPI model, considering aspects like data privacy, cybersecurity, and local contexts.
Evaluate the impact of DPI on financial inclusion, public service delivery, and economic development in India.
Examine India's role in promoting global digital public goods and shaping international standards for digital governance.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Support for Startup Ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) announced enhanced support measures for startups in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The initiative includes increased funding for incubators, mentorship programs, and easier access to venture capital for entrepreneurs outside major metropolitan areas.
• The aim is to decentralize the startup ecosystem, tap into local talent, and promote inclusive economic growth across India.
• Special focus will be given to startups in emerging sectors like AgriTech, HealthTech, and sustainable technologies relevant to regional needs.
• Workshops and hackathons will be organized to foster innovation and entrepreneurial spirit in smaller cities.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Startup India initiative launched in 2016.
DPIIT is the nodal department for promoting startups.
New focus: decentralizing startup ecosystem to Tier-2 & Tier-3 cities.
Support includes funding for incubators, mentorship, VC access.
Aims to foster innovation and create jobs locally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of fostering a robust startup ecosystem in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities for balanced regional development and inclusive growth.
Analyze the challenges faced by entrepreneurs in smaller cities compared to metropolitan hubs and how government policies can address them.
Evaluate the potential impact of startups in sectors like AgriTech and HealthTech on local economies and societal well-being.
Suggest a comprehensive strategy to nurture a thriving innovation culture and attract investment to emerging startup hubs.
GS3.imageDescription: Young entrepreneurs discussing ideas in a co-working space in a smaller city.
GS3.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world.
